subject: english
learning_objectives: Define a narrative essay and explain its key characteristics
Use imagination to paint vivid pictures in words
Write a narrative essay with a clear sequence of events

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: A narrative essay is one that requires the writer to relate an event or incident as an eyewitness would. It requires imagination.

sections.heading: Key Characteristics
sections.content:
• It tells a story or recounts an incident.
• The writer uses first-person perspective to create authenticity.
• Events are presented in a logical sequence.
• The language is vivid and imaginative, painting a picture in words.
• Sensory details (sight, sound, feeling) make the narrative come alive.

sections.heading: Sample Narrative Passage
sections.content:
It was pitch-dark outside the bedroom. It must have been that the security light was not switched on before we went to bed. There was some movement outside one of the windows. I listened. As I got up from bed to check at the windows, a flash of light through the window dazzled my eyes. I shouted 'thief' and the next thing I heard was a gun shot.

Note: The writer is trying to paint a picture in words. It is only by imagination that this picture can be made more beautiful.
